coptic.feast.1.brief: The Coptic New Year пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ 1 Thout, colored red for the blood of the martyrs

coptic.feast.1.theme: New Year of the Martyrs пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ remembrance of those who died for faith
coptic.feast.2.brief: The Cross found пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ bonfires mirror the flames Helena lit to find the buried wood
coptic.feast.2.name: Feast of the Cross
coptic.feast.2.practice: Lighting bonfires (demera), singing hymns, outdoor celebration on 17 Thout
coptic.feast.2.theme: Discovery of the True Cross by Empress Helena in Jerusalem

coptic.feast.3.practice: Midnight liturgy (29 Koiak / January 7), breaking the fast with festive foods
coptic.feast.3.theme: The Nativity of Christ пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ culmination of the 43-day Advent fast
coptic.feast.4.brief: God revealed in water пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the dove descends, the voice speaks, the Trinity appears

coptic.feast.4.theme: Theophany пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the Baptism of Christ in the Jordan, God revealed
coptic.feast.5.brief: The angel's greeting пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the moment when heaven asked earth's permission
coptic.feast.5.name: Annunciation
coptic.feast.5.practice: Joyful liturgy on 29 Paremhat (April 7), special hymns to the Theotokos
coptic.feast.5.theme: The angel Gabriel announces to the Virgin Mary пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the Incarnation begins
coptic.feast.6.brief: The Resurrection пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ death defeated, the stone rolled away, the first light of Sunday
coptic.feast.6.name: Easter
coptic.feast.6.practice: 55-day Great Lent, midnight Paschal liturgy, breaking fast, red eggs, greeting 'Christ is Risen'
coptic.feast.6.theme: The Resurrection of Christ пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the feast of feasts, joy of joys
coptic.month.1.brief: Month of Thoth пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the ancient scribe god opens the new year of the martyrs
coptic.month.1.liturgy: Start of the Coptic year, commemorating the martyrs

coptic.month.11.name: Epip

coptic.month.12.name: Mesori

coptic.month.13.liturgy: Days of prayer and reflection between years, no fasting
coptic.month.13.name: Nasie
coptic.month.13.origin: Epagomenal days (Greek epagomenai), outside the regular months
coptic.month.2.brief: Month of Paopi пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the sacred festival that honored the river's gift
coptic.month.2.name: Paopi
coptic.month.2.origin: From Egyptian Ipet (Opet festival), sacred barque procession
coptic.month.3.brief: Month of Hathor пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the cow-goddess of joy gives way to Advent expectation

coptic.month.3.name: Hathor

coptic.month.9.name: Pashons

ethiopian.month.5.brief: Fifth month пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the sun blazes and Timkat processions fill the streets with color

ethiopian.month.9.season: Main rains approaching, strong vegetative growth

korean.element.0.brief: Wood пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the green force that pushes upward through all obstacles

korean.element.1.brief: Fire пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the radiance that illuminates and transforms
korean.element.1.meaning: Transformation, passion, propriety, the force that spreads
korean.element.2.brief: Earth пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the center that nourishes and grounds all things
korean.element.2.meaning: Stability, centering, trust, the force that holds
korean.element.3.brief: Metal пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the sharp edge that refines and lets go
korean.element.3.meaning: Precision, righteousness, clarity, the force that contracts
korean.element.4.brief: Water пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the deep stillness that finds its way through everything
korean.element.4.meaning: Wisdom, depth, adaptability, the force that descends
korean.festival.0.brief: Lunar New Year пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the most important holiday, when the whole nation returns home
korean.festival.0.name: Seollal
korean.festival.0.practice: Charye (ancestral rites), sebae (bowing to elders for blessings), tteokguk (rice cake soup for gaining a year)
korean.festival.0.theme: New beginnings пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ honoring ancestors, renewing family bonds
korean.festival.1.brief: Great Full Moon пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the first full moon of the year burns away bad luck
korean.festival.1.name: Daeboreum
korean.festival.1.practice: Cracking nuts (bureom), eating five-grain rice (ogokbap), moon-viewing, fire festivals (daljip burning)
korean.festival.1.theme: Fullness пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the first full moon, wishing for a bountiful year
korean.festival.2.brief: Double Third пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the swallows return from the south and spring declares itself
korean.festival.2.name: Samjinnal
korean.festival.2.practice: Flower pancakes (hwajeon), spring outings, welcoming the swallows
korean.festival.2.theme: Spring arrival пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ swallows return, nature awakens
korean.festival.3.brief: Double Fifth пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ when heaven's yang energy is strongest and the earth celebrates with wrestling
korean.festival.3.name: Dano
korean.festival.3.practice: Ssireum (wrestling), swing riding, washing hair in iris water, surichwitteok (herb rice cakes)
korean.festival.3.theme: Peak yang energy пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the most yang day of the year (5th day, 5th month)
korean.festival.4.brief: Star Festival пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the celestial lovers' once-a-year meeting across the bridge of magpies
korean.festival.4.name: Chilseok
korean.festival.4.practice: Wishing upon stars, wheat flour noodles (milguksu), bathing in star-blessed rain
korean.festival.4.theme: Love and reunion пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the Weaver and Cowherd cross the Milky Way
korean.festival.5.brief: Harvest Moon пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ Korea's Thanksgiving, when the full autumn moon shines on shared abundance
korean.festival.5.name: Chuseok
korean.festival.5.practice: Songpyeon (half-moon rice cakes), charye (ancestral rites), visiting graves (seongmyo), folk games
korean.festival.5.theme: Gratitude пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ harvest thanksgiving, abundance shared with ancestors
korean.festival.6.brief: Double Ninth пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ ascending to high ground to see far, drinking the flower of longevity
korean.festival.6.name: Jungyangjeol
korean.festival.6.practice: Climbing mountains, chrysanthemum wine (gukhwaju), chrysanthemum pancakes, poetry
korean.festival.6.theme: Longevity пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ ascending heights, chrysanthemum appreciation
lao.festival.1.brief: Pi Mai пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ three days when Laos washes away the old year: Sangkhan Luang (farewell), Sangkhan Nao (the day between), Sangkhan Khuen (new dawn)

lao.festival.1.practice: Pouring water over Buddha images and elders, baci string-tying ceremony, sand stupas at temples, Nang Sangkhan maiden procession in Luang Prabang
lao.festival.1.theme: Water, renewal, baci пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the Lao New Year cleansing
lao.festival.2.brief: National Day пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ honoring the establishment of the Lao PDR and the long path to sovereignty
lao.festival.2.name: National Day
lao.festival.2.practice: National ceremonies, public holiday, reflection on independence (December 2, 1975)
lao.festival.2.theme: Sovereignty, memory пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ founding of the Lao People's Democratic Republic
lao.festival.3.brief: Liberation Day пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the moment Laos declared its independence from colonial administration
lao.festival.3.name: Liberation Day
lao.festival.3.practice: Ceremonies and remembrance of Lao Issara independence declaration (October 12, 1945)
lao.festival.3.theme: Freedom, history пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ liberation from French colonial rule
lao.month.1.brief: January пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Capricorn, the cool season wraps the river valleys
lao.month.1.season: Cool season peak пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the sea-goat month, Mekong mists, quiet temples

lao.month.11.brief: November пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Scorpio, the nation gathers at That Luang in Vientiane
lao.month.11.season: Cool season begins пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the scorpion month, Boun That Luang at the great stupa
lao.month.12.brief: December пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Sagittarius, Laos celebrates its founding and the cool returns
lao.month.12.season: Cool season пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the archer month, National Day (December 2), year closing
lao.month.2.brief: February пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Aquarius, monks chant the tale of the Buddha's last birth
lao.month.2.season: Cool season fading пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the water-bearer month, Boun Pha Vet (Vessantara Jataka)
lao.month.3.brief: March пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Pisces, the land waits between harvests
lao.month.3.season: Hot season begins пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the fish month, dry heat over rice paddies
lao.month.4.brief: April пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Aries, Nang Sangkhan descends as Laos washes away the old year
lao.month.4.season: Hot season peak пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the ram month, Pi Mai Lao water festival
lao.month.5.brief: May пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Taurus, villages launch rockets to summon the monsoon
lao.month.5.season: Hot season end пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the bull month, Boun Bang Fai rockets call the rain
lao.month.6.brief: June пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Gemini, the Buddha's birth, awakening, and parinibbana honored
lao.month.6.season: Rainy season begins пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the twins month, Visakha Boucha (triple anniversary)
lao.month.7.brief: July пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Cancer, monks enter the three-month rains retreat
lao.month.7.season: Rainy season пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the crab month, Khao Phansa (Buddhist Lent begins)
lao.month.8.brief: August пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Leo, the monsoon feeds the river and the paddies
lao.month.8.season: Rainy season peak пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the lion month, rice transplanting, Mekong rises
lao.month.9.brief: September пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Virgo, offerings placed at temples for the departed
lao.month.9.season: Late rainy season пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the maiden month, Khao Phadap Din (honoring ancestors)

persian.festival.1.brief: The oldest continuously celebrated holiday on Earth пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ 3000+ years of welcoming spring
persian.festival.1.name: Nowruz
persian.festival.1.practice: Haft Sin table with seven symbolic items, visiting family, wearing new clothes, goldfish and sprouted wheat
persian.festival.1.theme: New Day пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ rebirth of the world at the vernal equinox
persian.festival.2.brief: Nature Day пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ leaving bad luck behind by spending the day under the open sky
persian.festival.2.name: Sizdah Bedar
persian.festival.2.practice: Picnicking outdoors, tying grass knots for wishes, releasing the sprouted sabzeh into running water
persian.festival.2.theme: Thirteenth Outside пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ spending the 13th day of the new year in nature
persian.festival.3.brief: The night of Yalda пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ red fruits against the darkness, poetry against silence
persian.festival.3.name: Yalda
persian.festival.3.practice: Staying awake all night with family, eating pomegranates and watermelon, reading Hafez poetry (fal-e Hafez)
persian.festival.3.theme: Birth of light пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the longest night of the year conquered by togetherness
persian.festival.4.brief: The fire festival пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ leaping over flame to burn away the old year's sorrow
persian.festival.4.name: Chaharshanbe Suri
persian.festival.4.practice: Jumping over bonfires chanting 'give me your red, take my yellow' (health for sickness), fireworks, spoon-banging
persian.festival.4.theme: Red Wednesday пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ fire festival, purification before the new year
persian.month.1.brief: Month of the guardian spirits пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the ancestors bless the new year's beginning
persian.month.1.meaning: Guardian spirits of the righteous пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the protective ancestors

persian.month.10.season: Early winter пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ solstice past, the creator's presence in stillness
persian.month.11.brief: Month of good mind пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the righteous thought that discerns truth from lie
persian.month.11.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of good thought, right thinking, and animals

persian.month.11.season: Midwinter пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ contemplation, the discipline of good thought
persian.month.12.brief: Month of holy devotion пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the sacred earth preparing to bloom once more
persian.month.12.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of the earth, devotion, and loving faith
persian.month.12.name: Esfand
persian.month.12.season: Late winter пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ Chaharshanbe Suri, fire leaping, spring anticipated
persian.month.2.brief: Month of best truth пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the cosmic order made visible in blossoming nature
persian.month.2.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of truth, righteousness, and holy fire
persian.month.2.name: Ordibehesht
persian.month.2.season: Mid-spring пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the earth warms, truth becomes visible in green
persian.month.3.brief: Month of wholeness пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the perfection of the waters that sustain all life
persian.month.3.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of water, health, and perfection
persian.month.3.name: Khordad
persian.month.3.season: Late spring пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ waters flowing, fullness of the season
persian.month.4.brief: Month of Sirius пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the star whose heliacal rising brings the longed-for rain

persian.month.4.season: Early summer пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ heat begins, rain prayers
persian.month.5.brief: Month of immortality пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the deathless force that lives in every green thing
persian.month.5.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of plants, immortality, and deathlessness
persian.month.5.name: Mordad
persian.month.5.season: Midsummer пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ heat at peak, plants in full power
persian.month.6.brief: Month of desirable dominion пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the just sovereignty that rules the sky
persian.month.6.meaning: The Amesha Spenta of metals, sky, and righteous power
persian.month.6.name: Shahrivar
persian.month.6.season: Late summer пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ harvest approaching, the kingdom of light
persian.month.7.brief: Month of Mithra пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the lord of the covenant who watches over every promise

persian.month.7.season: Early autumn пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ equinox, balance of light and dark
persian.month.8.brief: Month of the waters пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the cleansing rains that renew the parched earth

persian.month.9.brief: Month of fire пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the sacred flame that holds back the longest night
persian.month.9.meaning: The yazata of fire, the sacred flame of Ahura Mazda
persian.month.9.name: Azar

tamil.festival.2.name: Pongal
tamil.festival.2.practice: Boiling rice in new pots until it overflows (pongal = overflowing), decorating cows, kolam art, bonfires
tamil.festival.2.theme: Harvest thanksgiving пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ gratitude to the Sun, cows, and the earth
tamil.festival.3.brief: The celestial wedding пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ Madurai's great festival celebrating the goddess who married Shiva
tamil.festival.3.name: Chithirai Festival
tamil.festival.3.practice: Ten-day festival in Madurai, chariot procession, re-enactment of the divine marriage
tamil.festival.3.theme: Divine marriage пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ Meenakshi and Sundareswarar's cosmic wedding
tamil.festival.4.brief: The festival of lights пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ every lamp declares that darkness cannot endure
tamil.festival.4.name: Deepavali
tamil.festival.4.practice: Oil lamps, firecrackers, new clothes, sweets, oil bath at dawn, family gatherings
tamil.festival.4.theme: Victory of light пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ triumph over darkness, the return of righteousness
tamil.month.1.brief: First month пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the Sun enters sidereal Aries and the Tamil year begins
tamil.month.10.brief: Month of Pongal пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the harvest thanksgiving, the Sun's northward journey begins
tamil.month.11.brief: Month of the water-bearer пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ Maha Shivaratri, the great night of Shiva
tamil.month.12.brief: Last month пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ Panguni Uthiram (divine weddings), the fish rasi closes the year
tamil.month.2.brief: Month of temple celebrations пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the bull rasi, steadfast under the blazing sun
tamil.month.3.brief: The twins month пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the monsoon arrives and the parched earth drinks
tamil.month.4.brief: Month of Aadi пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the crab rasi, monsoon fullness, Aadi Perukku (river festival)
tamil.month.5.brief: Month of the lion пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ Avani Avittam (sacred thread renewal) and monsoon bounty
tamil.month.6.brief: Month of Navaratri пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ nine nights of the goddess, the maiden rasi
tamil.month.7.brief: Month of the scales пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ Deepavali season, the balance between light and dark
tamil.month.8.brief: Month of Karthigai пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the lamp festival, the Pleiades at zenith
tamil.month.8.season: Festival of lights, cool season begins
tamil.month.9.brief: Holiest month пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ Margazhi kolams (rice flour art) at dawn, the archer rasi
thai.festival.1.brief: The water festival пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ three days when Thailand washes away the old year with joy and reverence
thai.festival.1.name: Songkran
thai.festival.1.practice: Pouring water over Buddha images and elders' hands, water fights in the streets, building sand stupas at temples
thai.festival.1.theme: Water, renewal, respect пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the Thai New Year cleansing
thai.festival.2.brief: Chakri Memorial Day пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ honoring King Rama I who founded Bangkok and the dynasty
thai.festival.2.name: Chakri Day
thai.festival.2.practice: Ceremony at the Grand Palace, paying respects to past kings, national holiday
thai.festival.2.theme: Dynasty, gratitude пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ honoring the founding of the Chakri royal house
thai.festival.3.brief: Constitution Day пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ remembering the transition from absolute to constitutional monarchy
thai.festival.3.name: Constitution Day
thai.festival.3.practice: National ceremonies, public holiday, reflection on democratic principles
thai.festival.3.theme: Democracy, nationhood пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ commemorating Thailand's first constitution
thai.month.1.brief: January пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Capricorn, the cool season reaches its depth
thai.month.1.season: Cool season peak пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the sea-goat month, harvest of cool-season crops
thai.month.10.brief: October пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Libra, balance returns as the rains recede
thai.month.10.season: Rainy season ending пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the scales month, Lent ends (Ok Phansa)
thai.month.11.brief: November пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Scorpio, floating lanterns honor the river goddess
thai.month.11.season: Cool season begins пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the scorpion month, Loy Krathong festival
thai.month.12.brief: December пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Sagittarius, the cool season's gift
thai.month.12.season: Cool season пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the archer month, Father's Day (King's birthday)
thai.month.2.brief: February пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Aquarius, the last cool breath before heat
thai.month.2.season: Cool season fading пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the water-bearer month, Chinese New Year festivities
thai.month.3.brief: March пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Pisces, the transition from cool to scorching
thai.month.3.season: Hot season begins пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the fish month, dry heat building
thai.month.4.brief: April пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Aries, the hottest month cooled by Songkran's water
thai.month.4.season: Hot season peak пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the ram month, Songkran water festival
thai.month.5.brief: May пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Taurus, the earth waits for rain
thai.month.5.season: Hot season end пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the bull month, monsoon anticipation
thai.month.6.brief: June пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Gemini, the monsoon announces itself
thai.month.6.season: Rainy season begins пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the twins month, first monsoon rains
thai.month.7.brief: July пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Cancer, monks enter the rains retreat
thai.month.7.season: Rainy season пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the crab month, Buddhist Lent (Khao Phansa) begins
thai.month.8.brief: August пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Leo, the rains intensify
thai.month.8.season: Rainy season peak пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the lion month, Mother's Day (Queen's birthday)
thai.month.9.brief: September пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ named from Virgo, the rice paddies glow emerald
thai.month.9.season: Late rainy season пҝҪпҝҪпҝҪ the maiden month, rice fields green
